audio/scene-etiquette/etq-course.mp3.text: How to behave on the golf course — pace of play and respect for other players.

audio/scene-etiquette/etq-distracting.mp.text: Stay still and silent when others are taking their shot.

audio/scene-etiquette/etq-play.mp3.text: Player furthest from the hole plays first; honor goes to the previous hole's low scorer.

audio/scene-handicapping/hcp-overview.mp.text: A handicap is a numerical measure of a golfer's playing ability.

audio/scene-handicapping/hcp-calc-score..text: Strokes per hole are tallied and adjusted by stroke index.

audio/scene-handicapping/hcp-calc-handi..text: Average of best differentials, multiplied by 0.96.

audio/scene-handicapping/hcp-example.mp3.text: Step-through of a handicap calculation across 20 rounds.

audio/scene-fun/fun-howto.mp3.text: Don't take it too seriously — relax, enjoy the course, breathe.

audio/scene-fun/fun-friends.mp3.text: Conversation between shots, compliments after good play, shared lunch after.

audio/scene-playing/play-rules.mp3.text: The USGA Rules of Golf govern competitive play.

audio/scene-playing/play-par.mp3.text: Par is the expected number of strokes for a scratch golfer to complete a hole.

audio/scene-playing/play-scoring.mp3.text: Stroke play counts every stroke; match play counts holes won.

audio/scene-playing/play-other-scoring.m.text: Stableford, scramble, best-ball — alternatives to stroke and match play.

audio/scene-playing/play-playing.mp3.text: Tee shot, fairway shots, approach to green, putting.
